svg.title: FastAPI 3-layer architecture request lifecycle
svg.desc: An HTTP request flows down through API Layer (Routers), Service Layer (Business Logic), and Repository Layer (Data Access), with a response flowing back up. PostgreSQL connects to the Repository layer and ImageKit CDN connects to the Service layer.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What kind of HTTP requests does a FastAPI router handle?')
sendPrompt('What responsibilities does the FastAPI router layer have?')
g.text: HTTP routing · JWT authentication · Pydantic request/response validation
FastAPI dependency injection · rate limiting · 4xx error responses

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What business logic lives in the FastAPI service layer?')
g.text: Orchestrates use cases · applies business rules · voting & scoring logic
Transactions · domain events · calls external services

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does the SQLAlchemy repository layer manage PostgreSQL sessions?')
g.text: SQLAlchemy ORM queries · session management · unit of work pattern
CRUD operations · query optimization · connection pooling

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does PostgreSQL integrate with SQLAlchemy in a FastAPI app?')
sendPrompt('How does ImageKit CDN connect to a FastAPI service layer?')
